old_claim.note: Breech-loading Firearms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Maxim's recoil mechanism acted through the breech action by ejecting a spent cartridge and feeding the next round, making breech-loading firearm architecture the direct operating substrate.

source_shape.source_locator: The Power of Recoil section: PBS describes Maxim's recoil mechanism as ejecting the used cartridge, drawing in the next one, and letting one barrel fire automatically.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: PBS describes the Maxim gun as an automatic one-barrel weapon whose recoil cycle ejects a spent cartridge and feeds the next cartridge.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Ejection and feeding through the action are breech-loading firearm operations, so the source supports the breech architecture as an enabling substrate rather than a broad firearms context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: machine_gun remains enabled by breech_loading_firearms, now with source-backed evidence.
would_reject_if: The machine_gun node is later narrowed to hand-cranked volley guns or Gatling guns.
A stronger source shows the 1884 Maxim mechanism did not use a breech action to eject and feed cartridges.
The breech_loading_firearms node is narrowed away from rear-loaded cartridge firearm actions.
short_reason: Maxim's automatic cycle used breech-action ejection and feeding.
